title: thymus serpyllum f. stenophyllus
intro: Thymus serpyllum f. stenophyllus, also known as creeping thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant that is native to the Mediterranean region and is characterized by its narrow leaves and creeping habit. It has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The health benefits of creeping thyme include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and boosting the immune system, as well as its ability to act as a natural antiseptic and antibacterial agent. Creeping thyme can be prepared as a herbal tea, oil, or tincture, and is often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce inflammation.
